title: The Father The Son And The Holy Guest St

jokes.text: Medieval? Like Lord of the Rings?
jokes.setup: Teacher announces medieval festival
jokes.punchline: Student immediately references pop culture fantasy instead of actual history
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons character comedy - kids relating everything to pop culture rather than education

jokes.text: I shall pattern my reign on Enguerrand VII, Sire de Coucy!
jokes.setup: Martin gets assigned to be king in school festival
jokes.punchline: He immediately references obscure medieval historical figure with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - his nerdy enthusiasm for obscure historical details

jokes.text: Tralloo Trallay! Not so fast, Nelson. You're one of King Martin's guards.
jokes.setup: Nelson makes enthusiastic medieval exclamation
jokes.punchline: Gets assigned subservient role to the nerd he usually bullies
jokes.explanation: Great reversal of typical school hierarchy with Nelson having to serve Martin

jokes.text: You'll pay for this...! ...my liege.
jokes.setup: Nelson threatens revenge on the teacher
jokes.punchline: Has to add respectful medieval address, showing his conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson moment - his aggression constrained by the role-playing context

jokes.text: Bart, you'll be playing the village cooper. What kind of a knight is that? It's the kind of knight that makes barrels and isn't a knight.
jokes.setup: Bart assumes cooper is some kind of knight
jokes.punchline: Teacher's deadpan explanation that it's not a knight at all
jokes.explanation: Great deflation joke - teacher's matter-of-fact correction of Bart's assumption

jokes.text: Ay, caramba. Well, this stinks. But I bet Lisa got something even stupider.
jokes.setup: Bart's disappointed with his boring role
jokes.punchline: Assumes Lisa got worse, setting up the reveal she's queen
jokes.explanation: Sets up the sibling rivalry reversal that's coming

jokes.text: All hail the queen!
jokes.setup: Bart just complained Lisa probably got something stupider
jokes.punchline: Cut to Lisa being crowned queen, the best role possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and reversal of Bart's expectation

jokes.text: The ones made from potatoes? The very same.
jokes.setup: Teacher offers Lisa special French fries as royal privilege
jokes.punchline: Lisa's amazement that they're made from actual potatoes implies school food is normally artificial
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on awful school food quality through Lisa's surprised reaction

jokes.text: My coat of arms is a Spider-Man couchant on a field vert.
jokes.setup: Student describes coat of arms in proper heraldic language
jokes.punchline: Uses medieval terms for modern pop culture character Spider-Man
jokes.explanation: Clever blend of medieval terminology with modern references

jokes.text: You mean like you? Now, what's 'vert' mean? Say it means punching.
jokes.setup: Student explains 'couchant' means lying down
jokes.punchline: Nelson relates it to his own position, then wants 'vert' to mean punching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - everything comes back to violence and his current situation

jokes.text: My face! What drunken call girl will have me now?
jokes.setup: Willie gets rubber cement applied to represent plague
jokes.punchline: His concern is about his romantic prospects with prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Great Willie characterization - his crude concerns and low standards

jokes.text: Now, peasants, since the Middle Ages had no compact discs or Super Mario Men,
jokes.setup: Teacher explains medieval entertainment options
jokes.punchline: Refers to video games as 'Super Mario Men' showing adult disconnect with kid culture
jokes.explanation: Funny adult mispronunciation of familiar kids' entertainment

jokes.text: Wait! Let me take out my contact lenses! Pelt all you want, but revenge will be Willie's.
jokes.setup: Kids are about to pelt Willie as part of historical recreation
jokes.punchline: Willie's practical concern about his contacts, then ominous threat
jokes.explanation: Good mix of practical Willie and his vengeful nature

jokes.text: That was a stapler!
jokes.setup: Kids pelting Willie with medieval-appropriate items
jokes.punchline: Someone threw a modern office supply instead
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke - kids going too far with inappropriate projectiles

jokes.text: Dragon in the dungeon Maiden in my lair / There's potions in my wizard's pot / Now drink it if you dare. Lute solo!
jokes.setup: Student performing medieval-themed song
jokes.punchline: Calls for 'Lute solo!' like a rock concert
jokes.explanation: Funny anachronism mixing medieval music with rock concert conventions

jokes.text: Where is the tower? It's the back seat of my Merkur. There's 37 cents in the coin caddy and stay out of my diary!
jokes.setup: Lisa dramatically orders Bart taken to the tower
jokes.punchline: The 'tower' is just the back of someone's car with mundane details
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation from medieval grandeur to mundane car details

jokes.text: Soon we'll have revenge on those who mocked me. Also on those who were kind to me. And on the parents who took time from their busy schedules to make today such a success.
jokes.setup: Willie plotting revenge on those who mocked him
jokes.punchline: Escalates to include people who were nice and even supportive parents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie escalation - his petty vindictiveness spiraling to include everyone

jokes.text: I hate them all!
jokes.setup: Willie's revenge list growing to include innocent people
jokes.punchline: Simple declaration of universal hatred
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to Willie's escalating rant with maximum economy

jokes.text: Scurry, me beasties... nibble and gnaw at their overstuffed buttocks!
jokes.setup: Willie releases rats as revenge
jokes.punchline: His dramatically villainous command to attack people's rear ends
jokes.explanation: Willie's theatrical villainy with appropriately crude target

jokes.text: Milhouse!, don't open your mouth!
jokes.setup: Rats are swarming the festival
jokes.punchline: Specific warning to Milhouse, implying he's particularly vulnerable
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse characterization - he's always the most vulnerable

jokes.text: Unlike all the other stuff I told you I didn't do, which I did.
jokes.setup: Bart claims innocence in the rat incident
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits to guilt in previous incidents while claiming innocence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - undermining his own defense through honesty

jokes.text: Well, don't expect to spend all week lying around on the couch like a bum, 'cause that's my thing!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Bart being expelled
jokes.punchline: Claims couch laziness as his exclusive territory
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - completely missing parenting opportunity to defend his laziness

jokes.text: Now we have to find another school for you. And if you get kicked out of that one, you're going straight in the Army, where you'll be sent straight to America's latest military quagmire.
jokes.setup: Marge discussing school options for Bart
jokes.punchline: Escalates to military service in vague but ominous current conflict
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary disguised as parental threat

jokes.text: Anything's possible with Commander Cuckoo Bananas in charge.
jokes.setup: Discussion of possible military conflicts
jokes.punchline: Homer's nickname for whoever's in charge politically
jokes.explanation: Great Homer political commentary - both silly and pointed

jokes.text: Think of the leg up you'd have!
jokes.setup: Marge notes a school is for the blind
jokes.punchline: Homer sees it as an advantage for Bart, missing the ethical problem
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic obliviousness to moral implications

jokes.text: If we want to throw good money after bad, why don't we just get Grandpa that new pacemaker?
jokes.setup: Discussing expensive private schools
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests medical care for his father as equally wasteful spending
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Homer moment - treating his father's health as frivolous expense

jokes.text: I need two AA batteries. Now! Get 'em from the smoke detector. What if there's a fire? The smoke will wake us up.
jokes.setup: Grandpa needs batteries for his pacemaker
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular logic about fire safety
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with Homer's flawed safety logic

jokes.text: There, you don't just get bad grades-- you go to hell!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Catholic school discipline
jokes.punchline: Escalates academic consequences to eternal damnation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer perspective on religious education consequences

jokes.text: My pacemaker!
jokes.setup: Battery conversation from earlier
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's pacemaker presumably failing at dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Predictable callback to the battery setup, but effective timing

jokes.text: Classic Grandpa.
jokes.setup: Grandpa appears to have medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of his father's potential cardiac episode
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deadpan response to serious situation - maximum economy and character

jokes.text: Ay caramba, do the Bartman, etcetera, etcetera.
jokes.setup: Bart introducing himself at new school
jokes.punchline: Lists his own catchphrases and dismisses them as routine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Bart's own merchandised persona and catchphrases

jokes.text: Thanks for the history lesson, sis. These days we use a yardstick!
jokes.setup: Nun mentions old days of using rulers for punishment
jokes.punchline: Modern 'improvement' is just a bigger measuring stick
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about Catholic school punishment evolution

jokes.text: A yard's 36! Now you tell me.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to stay out of yardstick range
jokes.punchline: Gets the measurement wrong, showing his poor education while being punished for poor education
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Bart's ignorance causing the very problem being addressed

jokes.text: Well, I guess they'd help me hold the dictionaries up.
jokes.setup: Nun asks Bart to imagine nails in his hands like Jesus
jokes.punchline: Bart finds practical advantage in crucifixion hardware
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart logic - finding upside in religious suffering metaphor

jokes.text: I'm the real Jesus here!
jokes.setup: Bart being punished for wrong religious attitude
jokes.punchline: Declares himself the actual Jesus due to his suffering
jokes.explanation: Bold sacrilegious joke perfectly in character for rebellious Bart

jokes.text: Sean, you wanker... he says... repent of your wicked ways, or sod off. Then he gobbed in my face and turned back into a streetlight.
jokes.setup: Father Sean's religious vision story
jokes.punchline: Saint Peter speaks like an Irish thug and spits on him before revealing he's just a streetlight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of religious conversion story with crude Irish elements and absurd ending

jokes.text: Stealing pairs of what? Hubcaps? Reeboks? Human eyes?
jokes.setup: Father mentions St. Augustine stealing pears
jokes.punchline: Bart mishears and escalates to increasingly serious theft
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Bart's criminal imagination running wild

jokes.text: Billy and Joseph start their penance at the same time. If each swear word brings a thousand years in Hell...
jokes.setup: Math class word problems
jokes.punchline: Catholic school makes math problems about eternal damnation calculations
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of Catholic education mixing academics with guilt

jokes.text: I wish I'd gone to more orgies!
jokes.setup: St. Sebastian being martyred with arrows
jokes.punchline: His deathbed regret is not attending enough orgies
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of martyr stories with modern hedonistic regret

jokes.text: With all the sitting and standing and kneeling, it's like Simon Says without a winner!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Catholic mass ritual
jokes.punchline: Compares liturgy to children's game but notes pointlessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer observation - reduces religious ritual to absurd game analogy

jokes.text: No meat?! What do they eat, light bulbs?
jokes.setup: Learning Catholics don't eat meat on Friday
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion leads to absurd alternative suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food obsession leading to absurd conclusion

jokes.text: That's Latin, Dad. The language of Plutarch. Mickey Mouse's dog? No, Plutarch. He chronicled the lives of the Roman emperors.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about Bart's Latin grace
jokes.punchline: Homer confuses classical historian with Disney character
jokes.explanation: Good Homer ignorance joke with Bart showing newfound learning

jokes.text: I didn't need that new fact! Now I forgot who won Bud Bowl VIII.
jokes.setup: Bart sharing educational information
jokes.punchline: Homer's brain has limited space and prioritizes commercial trivia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer brain dynamics - rejecting education to preserve beer commercial knowledge

jokes.text: You've transformed breakfast into dinner. It's a miracle!
jokes.setup: Catholic church serving pancakes for dinner
jokes.punchline: Homer sees meal timing change as divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Good Homer food worship - seeing mundane change as miraculous

jokes.text: I just can't remember what to yell out when you win. Bingo. That's my favorite game. I just can't remember what to yell out when you win.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing bingo
jokes.punchline: Repeats exact same statement, showing his mental loops
jokes.explanation: Homer's repetitive thinking pattern showing his mental limitations

jokes.text: I masturbated eight billion times and I have no plans to stop masturbating in the future.
jokes.setup: Homer confessing various sins
jokes.punchline: TMI sexual confession with future intentions
jokes.explanation: Shock humor typical of later Simpsons - pushes boundaries but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: In your face, Lord!
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he's been absolved of all sins
jokes.punchline: Celebrates by taunting God directly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer hubris - immediately misusing religious concept

jokes.text: Right... and how do I join? Do I whale on some Unitarians?
jokes.setup: Homer learning he needs to be Catholic for absolution
jokes.punchline: Assumes joining involves violence against other denominations
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - reducing religious conversion to tribal violence

jokes.text: And you look like you've accepted someone as your personal something.
jokes.setup: Marge notices Homer's been converted
jokes.punchline: Vague reference to 'personal savior' showing her discomfort with religious language
jokes.explanation: Good Marge characterization - uncomfortable with explicit religiosity

jokes.text: Well, I'm not having another 12 kids. Marge, no one is saying 12. Nine, ten, tops.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about Catholic family size expectations
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'reassurance' is only slightly smaller huge number
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer completely missing the point

jokes.text: Under the weather? Or under the spell of a man in a pointy white hat?
jokes.setup: Marge makes excuse for family's absence
jokes.punchline: Ned's suspicious follow-up about the Pope
jokes.explanation: Good Ned moment - his religious paranoia with clever wordplay

jokes.text: The Romans have been separate from us since the Schism of Lourdes in 1573. And that was about our holy right to come to church with wet hair.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy explaining Protestant-Catholic divide
jokes.punchline: Historic religious schism reduced to trivial grooming dispute
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reduction of major religious conflicts to absurd minutiae

jokes.text: Sorry, Marge. They're just not our sort. Well, then, I'd like to speak with Jesus. I'm afraid he's gone native.
jokes.setup: Marge wants to reunite with family in heaven
jokes.punchline: Jesus has abandoned Protestants to hang out with Catholics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious satire - Jesus as cultural defector from his own followers

jokes.text: Her husband's sleeping with her sister. Who said that?!
jokes.setup: Catholic confession booth context
jokes.punchline: Gossip spreads even in supposedly private religious setting
jokes.explanation: Standard confession booth privacy joke

jokes.text: He cheated. He's got it written on his arm!
jokes.setup: Homer correctly answers religious question
jokes.punchline: Bart exposes his father's cheating method
jokes.explanation: Standard school cheating joke applied to religious education

jokes.text: Sorry, Mom, but this is a Catholic church. Chicks don't have any authority here.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to retrieve Bart from church
jokes.punchline: Bart uses Catholic gender policies to dismiss his mother
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on religious gender dynamics through Bart's newfound authority

jokes.text: Back off, Popey LePew!
jokes.setup: Marge confronting the priest
jokes.punchline: Combines Pope with Pepé Le Pew cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau insult showing Marge's anger and pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: I thought there was a monster in the potty.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions changing Bart's diapers for five years
jokes.punchline: Bart's excuse for extended diaper wearing
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart deflection with childish excuse

jokes.text: Don't have a cow, man / Have a piece of fish, oy!
jokes.setup: Bart exploring Judaism
jokes.punchline: Modifies his catchphrase for kosher dietary laws
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of Bart's catchphrase for Jewish dietary restrictions

jokes.text: the Western branch of American Reform Presbylutheranism.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy describing their denomination
jokes.punchline: Comically specific and made-up Protestant denomination name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Protestant denominational splits with impossible hybrid name

jokes.text: We've got Boston, South America, the good part of Ireland, and we're making serious inroads in Mozambique, baby!
jokes.setup: Homer boasting about Catholic global reach
jokes.punchline: Lists major regions then escalates to obscure African progress
jokes.explanation: Good Homer competitive boasting with escalating geographic claims

jokes.text: How do you like those croutons!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to show Protestants can be hip
jokes.punchline: Uses salad garnish as her example of coolness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her idea of being edgy is hilariously mild

jokes.text: Well, I guess lots of kids have imaginary friends.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions she chose Buddhism
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses her religion as childhood fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - dismissing major world religion

jokes.text: Well, except for... you know.
jokes.setup: Father worried about being worst priest ever
jokes.punchline: Obvious reference to priest scandal without saying it
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to priest scandals through implication

jokes.text: Mom, a religion isn't cool just because they've glommed on to some crappy rock band from the 1940s.
jokes.setup: Protestant church trying to be hip with rock band
jokes.punchline: Bart sees through the pandering and gets the era wrong
jokes.explanation: Good Bart wisdom about religious marketing with factual error for humor

jokes.text: It's all Christianity, people! The little stupid differences are nothing next to the big stupid similarities!
jokes.setup: Religious conflict between denominations
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out the absurdity by calling both differences AND similarities stupid
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lisa wisdom - perfect structure calling out religious tribalism while maintaining neutrality

jokes.text: Monogamous gays and stem cells?
jokes.setup: Discussion of real enemies Christians should focus on
jokes.punchline: Priest's list of contemporary conservative targets
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor about contemporary religious conservative concerns

tmdb_overview: Bart gets expelled from school and transfers to a Catholic school, where a hip priest named Father Sean tries to convert Bart and Homer to Catholicism, which worries Marge when she believes that Catholics do not go to the same heaven as Protestants and discovers that Catholic women do not use birth control.
